tier: H5 / H-e: HFS+ hard links (private metadata directory + inode + link chain), symlinks, and complex/hardlinked delete
host: Host pre-check: Apple diskdev_cmds 540.1 fsck_hfs/newfs_hfs (the portable source Apple ships, built with gcc in WSL Arch -- includes HardLinkCheck.o/dirhardlink.o). The S.A.K. HFS+ writer CLI (sak_hfs_writer_cli.exe) produced the links; fsck_hfs validated. macOS-kernel mount + native-Apple fsck_hfs is the certifying confirmation.
change: Implemented the H-e driver core: (1) hard-link CREATE -- the first link to a regular file keeps the file's CNID but moves it into the on-demand `\0\0\0\0HFS+ Private Data` metadata directory as iNode<CNID> (its data blocks stay in place), turns the original name into a 'hlnk'/'hfs+' alias, and adds the new alias; further links prepend to the doubly-linked chain and bump the inode's link count. Byte layout harvested from macOS Sequoia: inode reserved1@4 = hl_firstLinkID, BSDInfo.special@44 = linkCount; alias ownerID@32 = hl_prevLinkID, groupID@36 = hl_nextLinkID, special@44 = iNodeNum, mode = inode mode & ~0222, flags 0x0022 (ThreadExists|HasLinkChain). (2) SYMLINK CREATE -- a file with mode S_IFLNK 0xA1ED, type/creator 'slnk'/'rhap', the data fork holding the target path. (3) hard-link DELETE -- decrements the inode link count, stitches the prev/next chain around the removed alias, and when the last alias goes, removes the inode and releases its data blocks. CLI: create-symlink-image, create-hardlink-image, delete-hardlink-image.
scenario: newfs_hfs -v SAKH5 a 64 MiB HFS+ volume; S.A.K. creates /shared.txt (data), a symlink /link-to-shared -> shared.txt, then hard-links /shared.txt -> /hard1.txt and /hard1.txt -> /hard2.txt (inode link count 3). The delete suite then removes the links one at a time (count 3 -> 2 -> 1 -> 0, inode + block reclaimed), fsck-clean at each step.

evidence_fsck.host_result: '** Checking multi-linked files.' + '** Checking catalog hierarchy.' + '** The volume SAKH5 appears to be OK.' on the 3-link volume; still OK after deleting a link (count 2) and after deleting all links + reclaiming the inode (final empty volume).
evidence_fsck.negative_control_note: deleting a non-hard-link via the hard-link delete path is rejected ('not a hard link'); the catalog mutation engine is the same certified streaming load/edit/rebuild used for H2/H3/H4.

evidence_unit: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp: hfsFileSystemWriter_createsHardlinksAndSymlinks (symlink target read-back; hard-link create count 2 then 3; root listing has all aliases + symlink; consistency clean; non-hard-link delete rejected; delete count 3->2->1->0 with inode reclaim; consistency clean). Full local core suite green; clang-format / lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) / cppcheck (exhaustive) all clean.
kernel_mount: CERTIFIED 2026-06-27 in a macOS Sequoia VM (qemu/KVM recovery, hfs kernel). The S.A.K.-written image (sha256 3E6681C2849FF5590C9DB36EBA98044CBC496A92FB1F59AF74351093812CAFC1) was attached on SATA (bare HFS+ = /dev/disk0). The macOS kernel MOUNTED it ('Volume SAKH5 on disk0 mounted'); 'ls -li /Volumes/SAKH5' showed hard1.txt, hard2.txt and shared.txt ALL as inode 16 with link count 3, and 'link-to-shared -> shared.txt' (lrwxr-xr-x, symlink resolved). 'cat hard1.txt hard2.txt link-to-shared' returned the IDENTICAL shared payload three times -- the kernel reassembled both hard links to the one inode's data and followed the symlink to the same content (round-trip of a hard-linked tree). Then Apple's NATIVE fsck_hfs (version hfs-683.100.9) on /dev/rdisk0: '** Checking multi-linked files. ... ** Checking catalog hierarchy. ... ** The volume SAKH5 appears to be OK.' Evidence hfs.h5-kernel-mount-linkcount.png + hfs.h5-roundtrip-shared-data.png + hfs.h5-apple-fsck-PASS.png. The H5 lane flips to Yes. (Note: the S.A.K. reader itself does not yet follow hard-link aliases to the inode's data -- that reader-side resolution is a separate read-path follow-on; the macOS kernel performs the authoritative round-trip here.)
